title: fix: terminate app validation output
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Terminates validator JSON output with a newline on success and failure
- Prevents GitHub Actions output delimiters from joining the JSON payload
- Adds a regression assertion for newline-terminated output

Checks:
- npx biome check --write .github/scripts/app-validate-submission.js .github/scripts/app-submission.test.js
- node --test .github/scripts/app-submission.test.js

gist.headline: JSON gets breathing room
gist.blurb: The app validator now puts a newline after its JSON, as nature and shell parsers intended. GitHub Actions delimiters can stop trying to merge with the payload.
gist.summary: Updated the app-submission validator to newline-terminate JSON output on both success and failure, preventing GitHub Actions output delimiters from joining the payload, with a regression test to keep it that way.
gist.impact: App submission workflow output now remains cleanly parseable in GitHub Actions for successful and failed validation runs.
gist.visual_concept: Polli in a tiny maintainer tool belt gently places a line break between a glowing JSON scroll and a GitHub Actions-style workflow gate, while a small code-plant grows beside the terminal; the visual metaphor is giving machine-readable output its own clean space.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny tool belt, carefully adding a glowing newline to a JSON scroll before it enters a retro workflow terminal; a code-plant blooms beside it and the terminal glows prominently lime green (#ecf874). Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, dark purple (#110518) outlines, with subtle pixel-font text: "JSON\n".
